data.title: CZ: Hong Kong Should Can Rival US, UAE as Crypto Hub
data.description: According to Odaily, in an exclusive interview with South China Morning Post, Binance founder and former CEO, Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said that Hong Kong has the conditions to become a virtual asset center on par with the United States and the UAE, but the key is that regulators need to act quickly.CZ pointed out that the Hong Kong government has shown a clear acceptance of Web3. He suggested that Hong Kong expand the scope of the four currently limited tradable cryptocurrencies, namely Bitcoin, Ethereum, Avalanche, and Chainlink, and allow exchanges to decide on token listings on their own.

data.description:

Zhao outlined his vision for crypto’s decentralized future, stating he would focus on building “a privacy-preserving, sustainable decentralized exchange and an AI trading agent for simple execution” if [. .

data.title: US Bitcoin ETFs dominate spot trading as institutional demand surges
data.description:
29-8-2025 – United States-based spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds have captured a substantial portion of daily spot trading activity, highlighting the rising interest from institutional investors in cryptocurrency. These ETFs now account for billions in daily volumes, often reaching $5 billion to $10 billion on high-activity days and occasionally outpacing volumes on traditional crypto exchanges, according [. .
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/us-bitcoin-etfs-dominate-spot-trading-as-institutional-demand-surges/

data.title: US Banks Moved $312B in Chinese Drug Money, But Crypto Gets the Blame
data.description:

data.title: Trader boosts XPL tokens with massive long positions on Hyperliquid
data.description:
The recent whale action for XPL on Hyperliquid was linked to one trader, Techno Revenant. The trader admitted a fat-finger mistake, instead of a malicious order, but still ended up draining the available liquidity, for a total profit of $38M.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trader-techno-revenant-boosts-xpl-tokens/

data.title: U.S. Inflation Data and Consumer Confidence Index Set for Release
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. core PCE price index for July is scheduled to be released at 20:30 UTC+8, followed by the final August Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index at 22:00. Forecasts suggest a 0.3% month-on-month increase in the PCE price index, consistent with the previous month. If accurate, this would raise the year-on-year PCE inflation rate from 2.8% to 2.9%, marking the highest level since February and the third consecutive monthly rise. Economists indicate that persistent high inflation could limit the scope for further interest rate cuts later this year. Some analysts believe that only a significant increase in the August CPI inflation data, due in two weeks, might prompt the Federal Reserve to reconsider its rate cut plans during the meeting on September 16-17.

data.title: Ethereum Price Analysis: Key Support Levels Identified
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, cryptocurrency analyst Lennaert Snyder has identified critical support levels for Ethereum. Snyder suggests that the next significant support for Ethereum is at $4,270. If Ethereum can confirm holding this level, it may be a favorable point to consider long positions.

Snyder advises that if the $4,270 support fails, traders might consider short positions. Additionally, deeper liquidity is noted around the $3,950 mark, which could be an area of interest for long positions if reversal signals appear.
